'''刺国若比売'''（さしくにわかひめ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する神。[[天之冬衣神]]の配偶神で、間に[[大国主]]をもうける。
==概要== 古事記において[[刺国大神]]の子とされ、八十神たちに殺された息子の大国主を蘇生させ、同神に木の国に行くよう施した。
[[長野県]]辰野町の宮木諏訪神社境内に刺国若比売の神陵が存在する。
==系譜== [[刺国大神]]の娘で、[[布怒豆怒神]]が[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、[[大国主|大国主大神]]を産んでいる。
==関連項目== * [[天之冬衣神]] * [[刺国大神]] * [[大国主]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年7月30日 (日) 03:40 (UTC)}} '''刺国若比売'''（さしくにわかひめ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する神。[[天之冬衣神]]の配偶神で、間に[[大国主]]をもうける。
==概要== 古事記において[[刺国大神]]の子とさる。[[八十神]]たちに殺された息子の[[大国主神]]を見て嘆き悲しみ、[[高天原]]の[[神産巣日神]]に懇願し、遣わされた[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|𧏛貝比売]]・[[蛤貝比売]]と共に彼を蘇生させた。しかし再び大国主が殺されると、再び蘇生させ同神に[[大屋毘古神]]の木の国に行くよう施した。
[[長野県]]辰野町の宮木諏訪神社境内に刺国若比売の神陵が存在する。
==系譜== [[刺国大神]]の娘で、[[布怒豆怒神]]が[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、[[大国主|大国主大神]]を産んでいる。

'''刺国若比売'''（さしくにわかひめ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[女神]]。
==概要== 古事記において[[刺国大神]]の子とされる。[[八十神]]たちに殺された息子の[[大国主神]]を見て嘆き悲しみ、[[高天原]]の[[神産巣日神]]に懇願し、遣わされた[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|𧏛貝比売]]・[[蛤貝比売]]と共に彼を蘇生させた。しかし再び大国主が殺されると、再び蘇生させ同神に[[大屋毘古神]]の木の国に行くよう施した。
[[長野県]]辰野町の宮木諏訪神社境内に刺国若比売の神陵が存在する<ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>。
==系譜== [[刺国大神]]の娘で、[[淤美豆奴神]]が[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、[[大国主|大国主大神]]を産んでいる。

{{Shinto-stub}}

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 刺国若比売 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 刺国若比売 | 別名 = 佐斯久斯和可比売 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->国土神 | 陵所 = 宮木諏訪神社 | 父 = [[刺国大神]] | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[天之冬衣神]] | 子 = [[大国主神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[赤猪岩神社]]、[[宮木諏訪神社]]等 }} '''刺国若比売'''（さしくにわかひめ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[女神]]。
==概要== 『[[古事記]]』において[[刺国大神]]の子とされる。[[八十神]]たちに殺された息子の[[大国主神]]を見て嘆き悲しみ、[[高天原]]の[[神産巣日神]]に懇願し、遣わされた[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|𧏛貝比売]]・[[蛤貝比売]]と共に彼を蘇生させた。しかし、またも八十神たちの謀略によって大国主神が殺されたため、再び蘇生させて息子に[[大屋毘古神]]の木の国に行くよう施した。
[[粟鹿神社]]の書物『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』では'''佐斯久斯和可比売'''（さしくしわかひめ）と記述されている。
[[長野県]]辰野町の宮木諏訪神社境内に刺国若比売の神陵が存在する<ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>。
「刺国」は「標」を刺すことで、領有を表し、「若」は父の「大」に対する娘の意で、「命」がつかないのは巫女性を表すとして、名義は「国を占有する子の巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
==系譜== [[ファイル:Susanowo family tree.png|thumb|250px|須佐之男命から大国主神までの系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神]] [[刺国大神]]の娘で、[[淤美豆奴神]]が[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、[[大国主|大国主大神]]を産んでいる。
==祀る神社== * [[赤猪岩神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]） * [[宮木諏訪神社]]（[[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]）

{{Shinto-stub}}

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 刺国若比売 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 刺国若比売 | 別名 = 佐斯久斯和可比売 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->国土神 | 陵所 = 宮木諏訪神社 | 父 = [[刺国大神]] | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[天之冬衣神]] | 子 = [[大国主神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[赤猪岩神社]]、[[宮木諏訪神社]]等 }} '''刺国若比売'''（さしくにわかひめ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]。
==概要== 『[[古事記]]』において[[刺国大神]]の子とされる。[[八十神]]たちに殺された息子の[[大国主神]]を見て嘆き悲しみ、[[高天原]]の[[神産巣日神]]に懇願し、遣わされた[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|𧏛貝比売]]・[[蛤貝比売]]と共に彼を蘇生させた。しかし、またも八十神たちの謀略によって大国主神が殺されたため、再び蘇生させて息子に[[大屋毘古神]]の木の国に行くよう施した。
[[粟鹿神社]]の書物『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』では'''佐斯久斯和可比売'''（さしくしわかひめ）と記述されている。
[[長野県]]辰野町の宮木諏訪神社境内に刺国若比売の神陵が存在する<ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>。
「刺国」は「標」を刺すことで、領有を表し、「若」は父の「大」に対する娘の意で、「命」がつかないのは巫女性を表すとして、名義は「国を占有する子の巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
==系譜== [[ファイル:Susanowo family tree.png|thumb|250px|須佐之男命から大国主神までの系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神]] [[刺国大神]]の娘で、[[淤美豆奴神]]が[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、[[大国主|大国主大神]]を産んでいる。
==祀る神社== * [[赤猪岩神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]） * [[宮木諏訪神社]]（[[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==関連項目== * [[天之冬衣神]] * [[刺国大神]] * [[大国主]]
{{日本神話}}
{{デフォルトソート:さしくにわかひめ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]
{{Shinto-stub}}

(Ōnamuchi){{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 Apr..."

revision.text:
{{draft}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}
(Ōnamuchi){{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], he is said to be the son of Sashikoku no Okami . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by [[:ja:八十神|the eighty gods]], she grieved and prayed to [[:ja:カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the Yatogami, so he was resurrected once again and had his son go to the land of the tree of [[:ja:大屋毘古神|Ooyabiko]] . In the [[:ja:粟鹿神社|Awaka Shrine]] book " [[:ja:粟鹿大明神元記|Awaka Daimyojin Genki]] ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' . The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . 信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修 The name "Sashikuni" refers to the stabbing of a "marker" to represent possession, "Waka" refers to the daughter in contrast to the father's "Dai", and the absence of "Inochi" indicates a female shrine maiden, so the name can be thought of as "the child shrine maiden who occupies the country" 新潮日本古典集成　古事記 . == Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == Shrines == * {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * [[宮木諏訪神社|Miyagi Suwa Shrine]] ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also == * {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] == References == {{reflist}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }}{{基礎情報 日本の神|名=刺国若比売|神祇=[[国津神]]|全名=刺国若比売|別名=佐斯久斯和可比売|別称=|神階=|神格=国土神|陵所=宮木諏訪神社|父=[[刺国大神]]|母=|親=|配偶者=[[天之冬衣神]]|子=[[大国主神]]|宮=|神社=[[赤猪岩神社]]、[[宮木諏訪神社]]等}}'''Sashikuniwakahime''' is [[Kami|a goddess]] who appears in [[Japanese mythology]] . == overview == == Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of [[:ja:刺国大神|Sashikoku-]] [[:ja:淤美豆奴神|no-Okami, and was married to Ame-no]] [[:ja:布怒豆怒神|-Fuyugoro-no-Kami]], who was born when [[:ja:天之冬衣神|Omizuno-no-]] Kami married [[:ja:布帝耳神|Nutei-mimi-no]] -Kami, the daughter of Funudzuno-no-Kami, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] . * == footnote == {{脚注ヘルプ}}{{Reflist}} == Related item == * [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]]

{{draft}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}
(Ōnamuchi){{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}}[[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[ja:刺国若比売]] (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], he is said to be the son of Sashikoku no Okami . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by [[:ja:八十神|the eighty gods]], she grieved and prayed to [[:ja:カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the Yatogami, so he was resurrected once again and had his son go to the land of the tree of [[:ja:大屋毘古神|Ooyabiko]] . In the [[:ja:粟鹿神社|Awaka Shrine]] book " [[:ja:粟鹿大明神元記|Awaka Daimyojin Genki]] ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' . (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}}[[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[ja:刺国若比売]] (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yatogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:ja:カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yatogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again and had his son go to the land of the tree of [[:ja:大屋毘古神|Ooyabiko]] . In the [[:ja:粟鹿神社|Awaka Shrine]] book " [[:ja:粟鹿大明神元記|Awaka Daimyojin Genki]] ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' . The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . 信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修 "Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. 新潮日本古典集成　古事記 . == Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}, who was born when Omizuno-no- Kami married Nutei-mimi-no -Kami, the daughter of Funudzuno-no-Kami, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] . == Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == Shrines == * {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * [[宮木諏訪神社|Miyagi Suwa Shrine]] ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also == * {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] == References == {{reflist}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }}{{draft categories| [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }}

(2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}}[[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[ja:刺国若比売]] (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yatogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:ja:カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yatogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again and had his son go to the land of the tree of [[:ja:大屋毘古神|Ooyabiko]] . In the [[:ja:粟鹿神社|Awaka Shrine]] book " [[:ja:粟鹿大明神元記|Awaka Daimyojin Genki]] ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' . The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . 信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修 "Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. 新潮日本古典集成　古事記 . == Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] . == Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == Shrines == * {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * [[宮木諏訪神社|Miyagi Suwa Shrine]] ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also == * {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] == References == {{reflist}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }}{{draft categories| [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }}

She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yatogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yatogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again and had his son go to the land of the tree of {{ill|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Ooyabiko}} . The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . 信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修 "Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. 新潮日本古典集成　古事記 . == Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. 新潮日本古典集成　古事記 . == Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] . == Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == Shrines == * {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * [[宮木諏訪神社|Miyagi Suwa Shrine]] ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also == * {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] == References == {{reflist}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }}{{draft categories| [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Divinité japonaise]] [[Category:Déesse japonaise]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]][[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }}

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 刺国若比売 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 刺国若比売 | 別名 = 佐斯久斯和可比売 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->国土神 | 陵所 = 宮木諏訪神社 | 父 = [[刺国大神]] | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[天之冬衣神]] | 子 = [[大国主神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[赤猪岩神社]]、[[宮木諏訪神社]]等 }}
'''刺国若比売'''（さしくにわかひめ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において[[刺国大神]]の子とされる。[[八十神]]たちに殺された息子の[[大国主神]]を見て嘆き悲しみ、[[高天原]]の[[神産巣日神]]に懇願し、遣わされた[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|𧏛貝比売・蛤貝比売]]と共に彼を蘇生させた。しかし、またも八十神たちの謀略によって大国主神が殺されたため、再び蘇生させて息子に[[大屋毘古神]]の木の国に行くよう施した。
[[粟鹿神社]]の書物『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』では'''佐斯久斯和可比売'''（さしくしわかひめ）と記述されている。
[[長野県]]辰野町の宮木諏訪神社境内に刺国若比売の神陵が存在する<ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>。
「刺国」は「標」を刺すことで、領有を表し、「若」は父の「大」に対する娘の意で、「命」がつかないのは巫女性を表すとして、名義は「国を占有する子の巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。 [[Category:十七世神]]
== 系譜 == [[ファイル:Susanowo family tree.png|thumb|250px|須佐之男命から大国主神までの系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神]] [[刺国大神]]の娘で、[[淤美豆奴神]]が[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘の[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、[[大国主|大国主大神]]を産んでいる。
== 祀る神社 == * [[赤猪岩神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]） * [[宮木諏訪神社]]（[[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天之冬衣神]] * [[刺国大神]] * [[大国主]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{日本神話}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さしくにわかひめ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]]

{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}
(Ōnamuchi){{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29}}|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}}[[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[ja:刺国若比売]] (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yatogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yatogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again and had his son go to the land of the tree of {{ill|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Ooyabiko}} . In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaka Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaka Daimyojin Genki|4=en}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' . The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . 信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修 "Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. 新潮日本古典集成　古事記 . == Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] . == Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == Shrines == * {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * [[宮木諏訪神社|Miyagi Suwa Shrine]] ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also == * {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] == References == {{reflist}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }}{{draft categories| [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Divinité japonaise]] [[Category:Déesse japonaise]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]][[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] [[Category:17 spirits of the world]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }}

"Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. 新潮日本古典集成　古事記 . == Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaka Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaka Daimyojin Genki|4=en}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' . The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . 信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修 "Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. 新潮日本古典集成　古事記 . == Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again and had his son go to the land of the tree of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} .
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaka Daimyojin Genki|4=en}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>
[[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]]
== Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again and had his son go to the land of the tree of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} .
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaka Daimyojin Genki|4=en}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

[[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]]
== Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again and had his son go to the land of the tree of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} .
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaka Daimyojin Genki|4=en}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

(2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]]
== Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 刺国若比売 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 刺国若比売 | 別名 = 佐斯久斯和可比売 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->国土神 | 陵所 = 宮木諏訪神社 | 父 = [[刺国大神]] | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[天之冬衣神]] | 子 = [[大国主神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[赤猪岩神社]]、[[宮木諏訪神社]]等 }}
'''刺国若比売'''（さしくにわかひめ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において[[刺国大神]]の子とされる。[[八十神]]たちに殺された息子の[[大国主神]]を見て嘆き悲しみ、[[高天原]]の[[神産巣日神]]に懇願し、遣わされた[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|𧏛貝比売・蛤貝比売]]と共に彼を蘇生させた。しかし、またも八十神たちの謀略によって大国主神が殺されたため、再び蘇生させて息子に[[大屋毘古神]]の木の国に行くよう施した。
[[粟鹿神社]]の書物『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』では'''佐斯久斯和可比売'''（さしくしわかひめ）と記述されている。
[[長野県]]辰野町の宮木諏訪神社境内に刺国若比売の神陵が存在する<ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>。
「刺国」は「標」を刺すことで、領有を表し、「若」は父の「大」に対する娘の意で、「命」がつかないのは巫女性を表すとして、名義は「国を占有する子の巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[ファイル:Susanowo family tree.png|thumb|250px|須佐之男命から大国主神までの系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神]] [[刺国大神]]の娘で、[[淤美豆奴神]]が[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘の[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、[[大国主|大国主大神]]を産んでいる。
== 祀る神社 == * [[赤猪岩神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]） * [[宮木諏訪神社]]（[[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天之冬衣神]] * [[刺国大神]] * [[大国主]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{日本神話}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さしくにわかひめ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]]
== Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]]
== Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

"Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. == Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]]
== Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

== References == {{reflist}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Original pages]] }}{{draft categories| [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:17 spirits of the world]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }}

'''刺国若比売'''（佐斯久斯和可比売）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]。 {{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 刺国若比売 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 刺国若比売（サシクニワカヒメ） | 別名 = 佐斯久斯和可比売 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->国土神 | 陵所 = 宮木諏訪神社 | 父 = [[刺国大神]] | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[天之冬衣神]] | 子 = [[大国主神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[赤猪岩神社]] * [[宮木諏訪神社]] 等 }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} == 概要 == 『古事記』において刺国大神の子とされる。[[八十神]]たちに殺された息子の大国主神を見て嘆き悲しみ、[[高天原]]の[[神産巣日神]]に懇願し、遣わされた[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|𧏛貝比売・蛤貝比売]]と共に彼を蘇生させた。しかし、またも八十神たちの謀略によって大国主神が殺されたため、再び蘇生させて息子に[[大屋毘古神]]の木の国に行くよう施した。
[[粟鹿神社]]の書物『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』では'''佐斯久斯和可比売'''（さしくしわかひめ）と記述されている。
[[長野県]]辰野町の宮木諏訪神社境内に刺国若比売の神陵が存在する<ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>。
「刺国」は「標」を刺すことで、領有を表し、「若」は父の「大」に対する娘の意で、「命」がつかないのは巫女性を表すとして、名義は「国を占有する子の巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Susanowo family tree.png|thumb|250px|須佐之男命から大国主神までの系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神]] 刺国大神の娘で、[[淤美豆奴神]]が[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘の[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、大国主神を産んでいる。 == 祀る神社 == * [[赤猪岩神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]寺内） - 主祭神 * [[宮木諏訪神社]]（[[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]宮木） - 主祭神 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[天之冬衣神]] * [[刺国大神]] * [[大国主]] {{shinto-stub}} {{日本神話}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さしくにわかひめ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

[[粟鹿神社]]の書物『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』では'''佐斯久斯和可比売'''（サシクシワカヒメ）と記述されている。

「刺国」は「標」を刺すことで、領有を表し、「若」は親神の「大」に対する娘の意で、「命」がつかないのは巫女性を表すとして、名義は「国を占有する子の巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Susanowo family tree.png|thumb|250px|須佐之男命から大国主神までの系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神]] 刺国大神の娘で、[[淤美豆奴神]]が[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘の[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、大国主神を産んでいる。 == 祀る神社 == * 宮木諏訪神社（[[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]宮木） - 主祭神 * 赤猪岩神社（[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]寺内） - 主祭神 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[十七世神]] * [[出雲族]] == 外部リンク == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/577/ 赤猪岩神社｜鳥取縣神社廳（公式ホームページ）] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さしくにわかひめ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 刺国若比売 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 刺国若比売（サシクニワカヒメ） | 別名 = 佐斯久斯和可比売、刺国若比売命、刺國若比賣命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->国土神 | 陵所 = 宮木諏訪神社 | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 -->[[刺国大神]] | 配偶者 = [[天之冬衣神]] | 子 = [[大国主神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 宮木諏訪神社 * [[赤猪岩神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''刺国若比売'''（佐斯久斯和可比売、 刺国若比売命、 刺國若比賣命）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』において刺国大神の子とされる。[[八十神]]たちに殺された息子の大国主神を見て嘆き悲しみ、[[高天原]]の[[神産巣日神]]に懇願し、遣わされた[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|𧏛貝比売・蛤貝比売]]と共に彼を蘇生させた。しかし、またも八十神たちの謀略によって大国主神が殺されたため、再び蘇生させて息子に[[大屋毘古神]]の木の国に行くよう施した。
[[粟鹿神社]]の書物『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』では'''佐斯久斯和可比売'''（サシクシワカヒメ）と記述されている。
[[長野県]]辰野町の宮木諏訪神社境内に刺国若比売の神陵が存在する<ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>。
「刺国」は「標」を刺すことで、領有を表し、「若」は親神の「大」に対する娘の意で、「命」がつかないのは巫女性を表すとして、名義は「国を占有する子の巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Susanowo family tree.png|thumb|250px|須佐之男命から大国主神までの系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神]] 刺国大神の娘で、[[淤美豆奴神]]が[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘の[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、大国主神を産んでいる。 == 祀る神社 == * 宮木諏訪神社（[[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]宮木） - 主祭神 * 赤猪岩神社（[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]寺内） - 主祭神 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[十七世神]] * [[出雲族]] == 外部リンク == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/577/ 赤猪岩神社｜鳥取縣神社廳（公式ホームページ）] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さしくにわかひめ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

revision.text:
{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]]
== Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]
== References == {{reflist}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] }}{{draft categories| [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:17 spirits of the world]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }}

However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]
== References == {{reflist}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] }}{{draft categories| [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]-->[[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }}
