{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]]
== Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]]
== Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]
== References == {{reflist}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] }}{{draft categories| [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]-->[[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with templates in the wrong namespace]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }}

{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]]
== Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

(2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

[[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] }}{{draft categories| [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]-->[[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]] [[en:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }}

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]
== References == {{reflist}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] }}{{draft categories| [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]-->[[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]] [[en:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }}

{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

(2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

"Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
