{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Sashikuni Wakahime|Sashikuni Wakahime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. revision.text:
{{draft}} {{promising draft}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Sashikuni Wakahime|Sashikuni Wakahime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{promising draft}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Sashikuni Wakahime|Sashikuni Wakahime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

Wakahime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{promising draft}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Sashikuni Wakahime|Sashikuni Wakahime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

[http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>

"Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

"Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}}
{{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Sashikuni Wakahime|Sashikuni Wakahime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Sashikuni Wakahime|Sashikuni Wakahime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{complex}}{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

[http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Sashikuni Wakahime|Sashikuni Wakahime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Sashikuni Wakahime|Sashikuni Wakahime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]][[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{complex}} {{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] )
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> [[en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] [[ja:刺国若比売]] [[fr:Sashikuni Wakahime]] == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yaso Gods|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "Dai" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
