(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|12=draft}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=draft}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=draft}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=draft}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=draft}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi|12=draft}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=draft}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|12=draft}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en|12=draft}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=draft}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=draft}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|12=draft}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|12=draft}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=draft}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=draft}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==

She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=draft}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi|12=draft}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=draft}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|12=draft}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en|12=draft}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=draft}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=draft}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|12=draft}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|12=draft}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=draft}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=draft}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

(2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=draft}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=draft}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=draft}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=draft}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi|12=draft}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=draft}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|12=draft}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en|12=draft}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=draft}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=draft}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|12=draft}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|12=draft}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=draft}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=draft}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==

She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=draft}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|10=ja|11=カミムスビ|Kamimusubi no Kami|en|Kamimusubi|fr|Kamimusubi|12=draft}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=draft}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|12=draft}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿神社|Awaga Shrine|4=en|12=draft}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=draft}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=draft}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|12=draft}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|12=draft}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=draft}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=draft}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=draft}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 刺国若比売 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 刺国若比売（サシクニワカヒメ） | 別名 = 佐斯久斯和可比売、佐斯久斯和可比賣、刺国若比売命、刺國若比賣命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->国土神 | 陵所 = 宮木諏訪神社 | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 -->[[刺国大神]] | 配偶者 = [[天之冬衣神]] | 子 = [[大国主神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 宮木諏訪神社 * [[赤猪岩神社]] }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''刺国若比売'''（サシクニワカヒメ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』において刺国大神の子とされる。[[八十神]]たちに殺された息子の大国主神を見て嘆き悲しみ、[[高天原]]の[[神産巣日神]]に懇願し、遣わされた[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|𧏛貝比売・蛤貝比売]]と共に彼を蘇生させた。しかし、またも八十神たちの謀略によって大国主神が殺されたため、再び蘇生させて息子に[[大屋毘古神]]の木の国に行くよう施した。
[[粟鹿神社]]の書物『[[粟鹿大明神元記]]』では'''佐斯久斯和可比売'''（サシクシワカヒメ）と記述されている。
[[長野県]]辰野町の宮木諏訪神社境内に刺国若比売の神陵が存在する<ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>。
「刺国」は「標」を刺すことで、領有を表し、「若」は親神の「大」に対する娘の意で、「命」がつかないのは巫女性を表すとして、名義は「国を占有する子の巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Susanowo family tree.png|thumb|250px|須佐之男命から大国主神までの系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神]] 刺国大神の娘で、[[淤美豆奴神]]が[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘の[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、大国主神を産んでいる。 == 祀る神社 == * 宮木諏訪神社（[[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]宮木） - 主祭神 * 赤猪岩神社（[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]寺内） - 主祭神 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[十七世神]] * [[出雲族]] == 外部リンク == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/577/ 赤猪岩神社｜鳥取縣神社廳（公式ホームページ）] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さしくにわかひめ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

「刺国」は「標」を刺すことで、領有を表し、「若」は親神の「大」に対する娘の意で、「命」がつかないのは巫女性を表すとして、名義は「国を占有する子の巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Susanowo family tree.png|thumb|250px|須佐之男命から大国主神までの系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神]] 刺国大神の娘で、[[淤美豆奴神]]が[[布怒豆怒神]]の娘の[[布帝耳神]]を娶って産んだ[[天之冬衣神]]に娶られ、大国主神を産んでいる。 == 祀る神社 == * 宮木諏訪神社（[[長野県]][[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]宮木） - 主祭神 * 赤猪岩神社（[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]寺内） - 主祭神 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[十七世神]] * [[出雲族]] == 外部リンク == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/577/ 赤猪岩神社｜鳥取縣神社廳（公式ホームページ）] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さしくにわかひめ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. {{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort=[[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|qq=}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==
* [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] * {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .

[http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort=[[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==
* [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] * {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort=[[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( [[Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==
* [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] * {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

[http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}} ( [[Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort=[[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}} ( [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==
* [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] * {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}} ( [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==
* [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] * {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

[http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|qid=Q48744862}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|qid=Q48745417}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
