{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort=[[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|qid=Q48744862}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|qid=Q48745417}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}} ( [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==
* [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] * {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q38276815]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort=[[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|qid=Q48744862}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|qid=Q48745417}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}} ( [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==
* [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] * {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]
== References == {{reflist}}

[http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|qid=Q48744862}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|qid=Q48745417}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q38276815]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Sashikuni Wakahime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort=[[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|qid=Q48744862}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|qid=Q48745417}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}} ( [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Nanbu Town,]] [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] ) == See Also ==
* [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] * {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort=[[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|qid=Q48744862}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|qid=Q48745417}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>

This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .

{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />

(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort=[[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]]<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|qid=Q48744862}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|qq=|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|qid=Q48745417}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}}}
{{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}
(刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>
== Overview ==
In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}},  she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>

In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>

The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修</ref>

"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成　古事記</ref> .

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]]
She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

{{Eight generations of Izumo}}

* {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Tatsuno Town,]] [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] )

[[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]]
She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

{{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}
(刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>
== Overview ==
In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}},  she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>

In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>

The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修</ref>

"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成　古事記</ref> .

== Overview ==
In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}},  she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>

In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>

The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修</ref>

"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成　古事記</ref> .

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]]
She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

(刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>

In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>

The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修</ref>

"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|WD=Q48744862}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|1=Akaiiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|WD=Q48745417}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}}}
{{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}
(刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>
== Overview ==
In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}},  she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>

In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>

The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修</ref>

"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成　古事記</ref> .

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]]
She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], who was born when [[:en:Omizunu|Omizunu]] married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}},  she grieved and prayed to [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi no Kami]] of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>

In the [[:en:Awaga Shrine|Awaga Shrine]] book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>

The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . <ref>信州の神事　長野県神社庁監修</ref>
