revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:刺国若比売]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:刺国若比売]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:刺国若比売]]
{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|WD=Q48744862|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|1=Akaiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|1=Akaiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|WD=Q48745417|1=Sashikuni Ōkami}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province|1=Izumo Province}}. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi no Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|Awaga Shrine|en|Awaga Shrine|WD=Q11604651|lt=Awaga Shrine|1=Awaga Shrine}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|WD=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|1=Akaiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}} ( {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Nanbu Town,|1=Tottori Prefecture}} [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Tatsuno Town,|1=Nagano Prefecture}} [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} ) == See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} * {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} * [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|Awaga Shrine|en|Awaga Shrine|WD=Q11604651|lt=Awaga Shrine|1=Awaga Shrine}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>

This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|WD=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:刺国若比売]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:刺国若比売]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:刺国若比売]]
{{infobox deity|type=Shinto|children=[[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name=":0" />
(Ōnamuchi)<ref name=":1" />|father={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02" />|consort={{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|WD=Q48744862|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|1=Akaiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|1=Akaiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|WD=Q48745417|1=Sashikuni Ōkami}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province|1=Izumo Province}}. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi no Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|Awaga Shrine|en|Awaga Shrine|WD=Q11604651|lt=Awaga Shrine|1=Awaga Shrine}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|WD=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
== Family tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|1=Akaiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}} ( {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Nanbu Town,|1=Tottori Prefecture}} [[:ja:西伯郡|Saihaku District,]] [[:ja:南部町_(鳥取県)|Tottori Prefecture]] ) * {{ill|Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja|宮木諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Suwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ( {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Tatsuno Town,|1=Nagano Prefecture}} [[:ja:上伊那郡|Kamiina District,]] {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} ) == See Also ==

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi no Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|Awaga Shrine|en|Awaga Shrine|WD=Q11604651|lt=Awaga Shrine|1=Awaga Shrine}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|WD=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .

[http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>; {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|WD=Q48744862|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}|mother=|cult_centre={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|1=Akaiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|cult_center={{ill|Akaiiwa Shrine|ja|赤猪岩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaiiwa Shrine|1=Akaiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635823}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|parents={{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|WD=Q48745417|1=Sashikuni Ōkami}}|offspring={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}}} {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|en:draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売) was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province|1=Izumo Province}}. She is the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|title=Sashikuniō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sashikunio/|access-date=2024-03-22|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>, and the husband of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}} and the mother of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|last1=Atsushi|first1=Kadoya|last2=Tatsuya|first2=Yumiyama|date=20 October 2005|title=Ōkuninushi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=Atsushi|first=Kadoya|date=21 April 2005|title=Ōnamuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127|access-date=2010-09-29|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref> == Overview == In [[Kojiki|the Kojiki]], she is said to be the son of the daugher of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} . When she saw her son, [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]], killed by the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, she grieved and prayed to {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi no Kami|1=Kamimusubi}} of [[Takamagahara]], who sent her son , Akebihime and Hamagurihime, to revive him. However, Okuninushi was once again killed by the schemes of the {{ill|Yasogami|lt=eighty gods|ja|%E5%85%AB%E5%8D%81%E7%A5%9E|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|link=https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, so he was resurrected once again wenr to the land of {{ill|5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|6=fr|10=ja|11=大屋毘古神|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|lt=Ooyabiko|4=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} for protection..<ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.]</ref>
In the {{ill|Awaga Shrine|en|Awaga Shrine|WD=Q11604651|lt=Awaga Shrine|1=Awaga Shrine}} book " {{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} ," she is described as '''Sashikushiwakahime''' .<ref>{{ill|10=ja|11=粟鹿大明神元記|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|4=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}</ref>
The sacred tomb of Sashikoni Wakahime is located within the grounds of Miyagi Suwa Shrine in Tatsuno Town, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} . <ref>信州の神事 長野県神社庁監修</ref>
"Sashikuni" (刺国) indicates a claim of territorial ownership through the act of "piercing" or "marking" (刺す). "Waka" (若) refers to "daughter," implying a relation to her father "O" (大), meaning "great." The absence of the word "Mikoto" (命) suggests a shamanic or priestess-like connection. This phrase could thus be interpreted as "the daughter priestess who claims territory," indicating a young female shamanic figure with a role in asserting territorial claims. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref> .
== Genealogy == [[File:Susanowo_family_tree.png|thumb|460x460px| A genealogy from Susanoo-no-Mikoto to Okuninushi-no-Mikoto (according to the Kojiki). Blue is a male god, red is a female god]] She was the daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni_Okami|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}}, and was married to {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, who was born when {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|WD=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}} married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}, and gave birth to [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-Kami]] .
